Correct. Sure, the kidnapper has something the victim's family loves, but the family has something the kidnappers lost for. Please also consider this. How many buyers do the kidnappers have for the commodity they're selling? Doesn't that, by definition, make it a buyer's market? And wouldn't you normally say cash is king? Who's decided with the cash here? What change is this? Emotions and perceptions of loss. P.S. If someone in your family gets kidnapped, call in a professional. If you don't know one, call us and we'll refer you to one. Negotiating the kidnapping of a family member or a friend without a professional's help would be the equivalent of a heart surgeon performing open heart surgery on a family member. Possible? Yes. Smart? No. Now, to finish our discussion of leverage. We've talked about the fear of loss being the biggest influence on the idea of leverage. Cool! So why don't we just threaten to inflict loss to get what we want? Why don't we just hold the thread over their head? Leverage, like beauty, is in the eye of the beholder. And that eye, for them, is what's in their mind and contains their own vision of the future. Threaten to take something from someone and what's the actual emotional fear of loss that takes over? The fear of loss of autonomy, which is a deeper threat producing a backfiring effect that ends up being the opposite of what you wanted. You have to know what the other side sees in their head. What in the future are they comparing in their mind's eye? They are making some sort of comparison in their mind's eye, and it's based on perception of how this is going to play out in the future and the emotions of what matters to them. To change their perception of what the leverage is, the best set of tools are the emotional intelligence skills of hostage negotiators that you've learned in this course. Change the loss they are comparing, and you will have influence also known as leverage. Remember, if your counterpart is talking to you, then you have leverage. You may even have it if they aren't talking to you, but if they are talking to you, then they feel that conversation is an avenue to what they want, and you, by definition, have leverage.
